author: doll-agent
content:
Done. The fragility was that data_ptr lived in parallel with the mmap field — a future refactor (taking ownership of mmap, splitting the pointer into a separate struct, reordering fields) could break the unsafe invariant silently.

Fix: replace stored *const T / *mut T pointers with derived data_ptr() / data_ptr_mut() methods that re-compute from self.mmap.as_ptr() on each call. The pointer can no longer exist without going through the live mmap field, making the invariant structural.

Knock-on:
- as_slice / as_slice_mut drop the const fn qualifier (memmap2 as_ptr isn't const-callable). Mmap construction is runtime-only anyway, so const wasn't reachable in practice.
- Alignment check at construction uses a one-shot probe pointer.
- _mmap renamed to mmap since it's now used.

ferray-io: 93 tests pass, clippy clean. Public surface for slice/view/flush/shape is unchanged. Commit b629f5d4.
